title: [CI:BUILD] followup PR for fcos with podman-next
body:
Followup on #19477

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: The Cirrus linter is printing warnings

> The Cirrus linter is printing warnings

@mheon sorry, ignore this PR for now. It's just a placeholder. Thanks

@benoitf btw, I hope `quay.io/podman/fcos:podman-next` has not been announced anywhere yet. I'm running a lot of the image build tests on https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox so the current SHA image tags are almost certainly messed up for now. Once this PR is merged, I'll be deleting prior SHA tags from quay.
@lsm5 it's not advertised on podman desktop documentation. Some podman desktop developers will probably try ( for the one using macOS but it's ok if it's totally broken :-)

@cevich I replaced the cirrus fcos build task with another github action. GHA does provide some reusable workflows but for now the pre-merge action is simple enough to not worry about reusability. In the pre-merge action I only care about using the Containerfile as-is along with pre-existing rpms from podman-next and not worry about installing the pre-merge rpms built by packit on the PR.

this is what an image build and push action will look like after merge: https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/actions/runs/6113932428/job/16594431532 . Let's ensure it looks ok before merging.

@cevich PTAL . The `Build FCOS image` still shows some errors about adding tag to manifest, but doesn't seem to be a deal-breaker https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/actions/runs/6123537430/job/16621629075 . quay images are pushed just fine .
Still need to check if I can add annotations.

> Still need to check if I can add annotations.

will try this in sandbox first.

> @mheon sorry, ignore this PR for now. It's just a placeholder. Thanks

Maybe convert the PR into a draft, so it's more obvious that it's still WIP and it will prevent us from accidentally merging.
@cevich PTAL. The source label has been fixed to point to the Containerfile for the SHA used.
@benoitf would you like `crun-wasm` pulled into these fcos images? I guess I could do that in a second followup PR. I'd rather not rerun all tests here since this PR should be good to go.

ah yes that would be awesome

`crun-wasm` and `wasmedge-rt`

and yes another PR will be fine

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/hold cancel

the github action failed at `wait-for-copr` https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/6247273398/job/16959529269#step:6:16 . Seems like a packit / copr issue. The packit team is investigating this. Once they have it fixed, our GHA should work as well.
ugh, the changes in `.packit.sh` for `podman --version` to show short_sha also don't seem to have taken effect, don't know why. Will work on that in yet another followup.

> the github action failed at wait-for-copr

When it's fixed, it might be worth considering if you should pin onto that working version somehow.  The caution here is, don't have any hope of managing updates to that with Renovate, since wait-for-copr isn't tested in the PR workflow.

> ugh, the changes in .packit.sh for podman --version to show short_sha also don't seem to have taken effect

Oh man, sorry :cry: Let me guess, that isn't/wasn't something you could test in the sandbox?

> Will work on that in yet another followup.

These kinds of problems are only amplified by not having full end-to-end testing in PRs.  Not sure how that could be done in this case though.  At least easily.

-sigh-

> When it's fixed, it might be worth considering if you should pin onto that working version somehow. The caution here is, don't have any hope of managing updates to that with Renovate, since wait-for-copr isn't tested in the PR workflow.

ya, pinning will work. I can change that once it's fixed. And I guess renovate only works on go.mod. No use if the repo is only mentioned in GHA.

> Oh man, sorry 😢 Let me guess, that isn't/wasn't something you could test in the sandbox?

> These kinds of problems are only amplified by not having full end-to-end testing in PRs. Not sure how that could be done in this case though. At least easily.

What happened in this case was that I verified the short sha in `./bin/podman --version` by running make locally. I forgot to verify it using the rpms built by packit. So, this could have been noticed earlier had I remembered to look in the right place.

> > When it's fixed, it might be worth considering if you should pin onto that working version somehow. The caution here is, don't have any hope of managing updates to that with Renovate, since wait-for-copr isn't tested in the PR workflow.
> 
> ya, pinning will work. I can change that once it's fixed. And I guess renovate only works on go.mod. No use if the repo is only mentioned in GHA.
> 

@cevich hmm, in this case if it was a problem in [copr](https://github.com/fedora-copr/copr/issues/2819) itself and not `wait-for-copr` there's only so much pinning would do I guess.

> And I guess renovate only works on go.mod. No use if the repo is only mentioned in GHA.

Actually it has a really powerful mechanism to manage versions using regular-expressions.  The problem here is any update PR it proposed wouldn't actually run the thing being updated. 
So nobody would know if the update itself broke something.

> there's only so much pinning would do I guess.

It would prevent an upstream breaking-change (or broken change) from affecting automation.

> > And I guess renovate only works on go.mod. No use if the repo is only mentioned in GHA.
> 
> Actually it has a really powerful mechanism to manage versions using regular-expressions. The problem here is any update PR it proposed wouldn't actually run the thing being updated. So nobody would know if the update itself broke something.
> 
> > there's only so much pinning would do I guess.
> 
> It would prevent an upstream breaking-change (or broken change) from affecting automation.

I think I can do this once the issue is resolved on the copr side. So far, we've only had 2 successful image builds using wait-for-copr.
